finding_summary: m21-dog-tribal.md attributes three phrases to Rosewater's 'But Wait, There's Core' as verbatim ('small dog tribal theme verbatim', 'six canine creatures across the set', 'dog tribal theme') — none of those phrases appear in the article (two-shot WebFetch verification 2026-05-26). Rosewater's actual phrasing is 'small dog theme' (attributed to set designer Adam Prosak) + counts 'three other white dogs and three red dogs' beyond Pack Leader. Also: roster table calls Pack Leader #28 (Scryfall says #29), and the table omits Igneous Cur (M21-153) which the body itself names at line 45 as part of the Alpine-Watchdog tri-cluster.

fixes.before: canonical_source: "Mark Rosewater, 'But Wait, There's Core,' Making Magic, 2020-06-15 (introduces the M21 Hound-to-Dog rename + names the deliberate small dog tribal theme verbatim: Pack Leader as rare dog lord, six canine creatures across the set, Rin and Seri Inseparable as the buy-a-box capstone, art direction quoted as 'a variety of different kinds of dogs, many on the cuter side'); Scryfall M21 set page (filter `s:m21 t:dog` returns the six canonical canine creatures); MTG Wiki — Core Set 2021 set page (Cycles and Themes section confirms the dog subtheme); per-card type-line confirmation across Scryfall M21 per-card pages"
fixes.after: canonical_source: "Mark Rosewater, 'But Wait, There's Core,' Making Magic, 2020-06-15 (introduces the M21 Hound-to-Dog rename + attributes the 'small dog theme' to set designer Adam Prosak, naming Pack Leader as the rare 'Dog lord' and counting 'three other white dogs and three red dogs' beyond Pack Leader, plus Rin and Seri Inseparable as the buy-a-box capstone, with art direction quoted as 'a variety of different kinds of dogs, many on the cuter side'); Scryfall M21 set page (filter `s:m21 t:dog` returns eight native Dog-typed creatures including Rin and Seri); MTG Wiki — Core Set 2021 set page (Cycles and Themes section documents the dog subtheme); per-card type-line confirmation across Scryfall M21 per-card pages"
fixes.rationale: Removes the 'verbatim' claim on a phrase not in the article ('small dog tribal theme'), corrects Rosewater's actual attribution to Prosak, replaces 'six canine creatures across the set' (not in article, fabricated count) with Rosewater's actual count framing ('three other white dogs and three red dogs' = the W+R count beyond Pack Leader), and corrects the Scryfall filter return count (eight native Dog-typed creatures, not six).

fixes.before: Core Set 2021 (M21, released 2020-07-03) is the set where Wizards of the Coast officially retired the "Hound" creature subtype and replaced it with "Dog," errataing every previously printed Hound card retroactively. Mark Rosewater's "But Wait, There's Core" Making Magic article (2020-06-15) framed the change as "a fight I had for 20-plus years only to have someone else win it for me without any resistance" — set designer Adam Prosak pushed the rename through without internal pushback. M21 then printed a deliberate small dog tribal theme to anchor the announcement: Pack Leader as the rare dog lord, six canine creatures distributed across the set's commons and uncommons (one per color where flavor permitted), and the *buy-a-box* legendary promo **Rin and Seri, Inseparable** as the Dog-Cat-pairing capstone. The art team was directed verbatim to illustrate "a variety of different kinds of dogs, many on the cuter side."
fixes.after: Core Set 2021 (M21, released 2020-07-03) is the set where Wizards of the Coast officially retired the "Hound" creature subtype and replaced it with "Dog," errataing every previously printed Hound card retroactively. Mark Rosewater's "But Wait, There's Core" Making Magic article (2020-06-15) framed the change as "a fight I had for 20-plus years only to have someone else win it for me without any resistance" — set designer Adam Prosak pushed the rename through without internal pushback. M21 then printed a deliberate small dog theme (Rosewater's framing, attributed to Prosak) to anchor the announcement: Pack Leader as the rare "Dog lord", three other white dogs and three red dogs distributed across the set's commons and uncommons, and the *buy-a-box* legendary promo **Rin and Seri, Inseparable** as the Dog-Cat-pairing capstone. The art team was directed to illustrate "a variety of different kinds of dogs, many on the cuter side."
fixes.rationale: Replaces 'small dog tribal theme' (not Rosewater's phrasing) with the actual 'small dog theme' framing Rosewater used in the article, attributed correctly to Prosak. Replaces the fabricated 'six canine creatures' count with Rosewater's actual count language ('three other white dogs and three red dogs'). Removes 'verbatim' from the art-direction quote so it reads as a direct quote rather than a verbatim-attribution claim.

fixes.before: | 28 | Pack Leader | Creature — Dog | W | strict: the rare dog lord; Rosewater names it as the tribal anchor | no |
fixes.after: | 29 | Pack Leader | Creature — Dog | W | strict: the rare "Dog lord" per Rosewater; tribal anchor of the cohort | no |
fixes.rationale: Pack Leader's correct M21 collector number is 29 (Scryfall-verified), not 28. Also reframes Rosewater's actual phrasing 'Dog lord' as a quote rather than paraphrasing 'tribal anchor' as if Rosewater said it.

fixes.before: | 138 | Chandra's Pyreling | Creature — Elemental Lizard | R | excluded: not a Dog type-line; reptilian companion archetype; outside the dog cohort despite the named-Chandra-companion pairing with Magmutt | no |
fixes.after:
| 138 | Chandra's Pyreling | Creature — Elemental Lizard | R | excluded: not a Dog type-line; reptilian companion archetype; outside the dog cohort despite the named-Chandra-companion pairing with Magmutt | no |
| 153 | Igneous Cur | Creature — Elemental Dog | R | strict: Scryfall `s:m21 t:dog`; M21 native Dog-type print; named in Alpine Watchdog's tri-cluster mechanical lock | no |
fixes.rationale: Inserts the missing Igneous Cur row. The body's Alpine Watchdog bullet at line 45 already names Igneous Cur (M21-153) as part of the Alpine-Houndmaster-Alpine-Watchdog-Igneous-Cur tri-cluster, so the body knows the card exists; the roster table simply forgot to list it. Adding here keeps the table consistent with the body claim and brings the count to 8 native-Dog-typed Scryfall returns + Rin and Seri.

fixes.rationale: Removes the third instance of the fabricated 'six canine creatures across the set' attribution and replaces with Rosewater's actual count framing + the correct Scryfall return count.

verification_command: grep -n -i 'six canine\|small dog tribal\|tribal theme verbatim' cards/_characters/m21-dog-tribal.md && echo --- && curl -s 'https://api.scryfall.com/cards/named?exact=Pack+Leader&set=m21' | python -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print('Scryfall Pack Leader cn:', d.get('collector_number'), 'rarity:', d.get('rarity'))" && echo --- && curl -s 'https://api.scryfall.com/cards/named?exact=Igneous+Cur&set=m21' | python -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print('Scryfall Igneous Cur cn:', d.get('collector_number'))"
uncertainty_notes: The card-level trivia bullet on Rambunctious Mutt (cards/magic-the-gathering/core-set-2021/30-rambunctious-mutt.md line 86) carries the same 'small dog tribal theme' + 'six canine creatures across the set' fabrication and cites Rosewater. That's the upstream source the m21-dog-tribal node imported from. NOT included in this fix-diff to keep scope tight to the node; future janitor pass should reconcile Rambunctious Mutt's trivia bullet against Rosewater's actual phrasing. Other card-level trivia bullets (Selfless Savior, Bolt Hound, Chandra's Magmutt) cite Rosewater more loosely and do not repeat the specific fabricated phrases.
